# Derb — Understanding Morocco

> An urban reference explaining the everyday realities of Morocco's cities — infrastructure, climate, culture — without tourism language. 57 questions answered, 7 thematic guides. Published by Slow Morocco, a Dancing with Lions project.

## About

Derb is an independent urban reference for Morocco. It explains the things visitors notice but don't understand: why riads feel cooler than outside, why there are cockroaches, why Google Maps fails in the medina, why the bathroom smells, why time feels different. Each answer goes beyond surface observation into the infrastructure, architecture, climate science, or cultural system behind it.

The name "derb" (درب) means alley or lane in Moroccan Arabic — the narrow passages that form the circulatory system of every medina.

- Staying Safe: https://derb.so/guides/staying-safe
- Religious Calendar: https://derb.so/guides/religious-calendar
- The Cities: https://derb.so/guides/the-cities
